webLinks.linkText: White Bay Power Station - Biennale of Sydney

properties.publicTransport: Route 441 and 442 buses run regularly to Rozelle. As well, route 443 heritage buses will run on weekends and public holidays. See details at transportnsw.info
properties.otherAdvice:
The Biennale of Sydney will be held at White Bay Power Station open from Tuesday-Sundays 10am-5pm daily, from Saturday 14 March to Sunday 14 June. The exhibition will also be open on public holidays.

Traffic will be heavy on roads approaching White Bay Power Station as crowds travel to and from the event,

Attendees driving should carpool with their group, plan to arrive early as local parking is limited.

Motorists should allow extra travel time on Victoria Rd, the City West Link and Robert St.

properties.publicTransport: Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
March shows at the TikTok Entertainment Centre:

  • Jimmy Carr from Friday 13 to Sunday 15 March. Shows start nightly at 7.30pm
  • Big Time Rush on Tuesday 17 March. Shows starts 7.30pm
  • Old Dominion on Wednesday 18 March. Show starts at 7pm
  • Mel Robbins on Wednesday 25 March. Show starts at 7.30pm
  • Jeff Chang on Saturday 28 March. Show starts 7.30pm

Traffic may be heavier than usual as attendees travel to and from the event.

Motorists should allow extra travel time and be aware of increased pedestrian activity on local roads.

properties.publicTransport: Ferries will be busier than usual while the event is underway, allow extra travel time. Additional buses will run. Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
The Royal Australian Navy International Fleet Review will be held across Sydney Harbour on Saturday 21 March. The Exercise Kakadu Fleet Review 2026 will see the largest gathering of international warships on Sydney Harbour for more than a decade with up to 30 ships from 20 countries assembling in an impressive display of international sea power. 

There are various vantage points available north and south of the harbour to see the navy vessels as they arrive, anchor and leave the harbour, throughout the day.  

  • Traffic may be heavier than usual on roads as crowds gather to see the navy vessels from land.
  • Special event clearways will be in effect from 6am Saturday 21 March to 6am Wednesday 25 March on Cowper Wharf Roadway western side between Dowling St and Wylde St

Clearways are strict no parking zones for all vehicle types, and apply even to local residents and businesses with parking permits or TfNSW Mobility Scheme permit holders. If your vehicle is towed, you can search your vehicle registration at the Find My Car website to find its new location, or alternatively call 132 701 to speak to an operator.

Special event clearways will be in place continuously from 2am Sat 28 Mar to 2am Mon 30 Mar at the following locations to facilitate rail replacement bus operations for Metro North West trackwork:

  • Railway St, southbound between Day St & Help St,
  • Railway St, northbound between Brown St & Help St
  • Brown St, westbound between Railway St & Pacific Hwy.

Special event clearways are strict no-parking zones and also apply to local residents and Mobility Scheme Parking Permit holders. Vehicles left in clearways will be towed and a fee applies. If your vehicle is towed, you can search your vehicle registration at the Find My Car website to find its new location or alternatively call 132 701 to speak to an operator.
properties.displayName: SPECIAL EVENT CLEARWAYS Sydney Trains Trackwork

properties.publicTransport: Route 389 and 501 buses will be diverting around road closures. Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
Road closures and special event clearways will be in place for the City of Sydney's Sydney Streets event on Harris St. The event runs from 11am until 10pm. 

Motorists in the area should allow extra travel time, follow the directions of traffic controllers and be aware of increased pedestrian activity.

Road closures will be in place from 7am until 11:30pm on:

  • Harris St between Pyrmont Bridge Rd and John St
  • Miller St between Little Mount St and Harris St
  • Sections of other local roads will also be closed including Bulwara Rd, Jones St, Little Mount St, Miller Ln, Saunders St and Union St.

Special event clearways will be in effect from 7am until 11:30pm on sections of Harris St and Miller St. 

Clearways are strict no-parking zones for all vehicle types, and apply even to local residents and TfNSW Mobility Scheme Permit holders. Check signs carefully as vehicles left in clearways will be towed and a fee applies. If your vehicle is towed, you can search your vehicle registration at the Find My Car website to find its new location, or alternatively call 132 701 to speak to an operator.

arrangementElements.html:

  • Jones St* Off Miller St

arrangementElements.title: Road closures & Special event clearways

properties.publicTransport: Major trackwork is taking place on Sunday 29 March on the T1 North Shore Line, Sydney Metro line (between Victoria Cross and Macquarie Park) and the City Circle. Use Light rail services or Metro to get within walking distance of the start line. Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
Run2Cure fun run events will take place in The Domain starting from 6.30am finishing around 1pm. Approximately 3,500 participants are expected.

Traffic may be heavier than usual on roads approaching The Domain as participants travel to and from the event.

Road closures: Art Gallery Rd and Mrs Macquaries Rd will be closed from 5am to 11.30am. No vehicle access or parking will be permitted until roads are reopened.

Hospital Rd will remain open with limited parking and a drop off zone located immediately south of the pedestrian crossing on the corner of Hospital Rd and Phillip Path. Parking is available in the Domain car park.

properties.publicTransport: Major trackwork is taking place on Sunday 29 March on the T1 North Shore Line, Sydney Metro line (between Victoria Cross and Macquarie Park) and the City Circle. Bikes are not permitted to be taken on-board buses including rail replacement buses. Trains continue to run to Hornsby on the T9 Northern Line. Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
Up to 3,000 participants are expected to take part in the annual Bobbin Head Cycle Classic. Events start and finish at Karuah Oval in Turramurra, from 6.30am. All events will finish by 1.30pm. Changed traffic conditions will be in effect along the course for the safety of participants.

  • Road and lane closures will be in place along the course at various locations and times between 5am and 3pm, including on sections of Karuah Rd, Turramurra Ave, Bobbin Head Rd, Warrimoo Ave, Woodbury Rd, Liberator General San Martin Dr, Old Pacific Hwy, Mona Vale Rd and Burns Rd through Turramurra, Ku-Ring-Gai Chase National Park, Mt Colah, St Ives and Terrey Hills. 
  • Speed limit reductions will be in effect at key locations along the course at various times between 6am and 2pm.

A special event clearway will also be in place on the eastern side of Mona Vale Rd in St Ives from 7am until 1.30pm, between 425 Mona Vale Rd and Kitchener St.

Clearways are strict no-parking zones for all vehicle types, and apply even to local residents and TfNSW Mobility Scheme Permit holders. Check signs carefully as vehicles left in clearways will be towed and a fee applies. If your vehicle is towed, you can search your vehicle registration at the Find My Car website to find its new location, or alternatively call 132 701 to speak to an operator.

SPECIAL EVENT CLEARWAYS

If your vehicle is towed, you can search your vehicle registration at the Find My Car website to find its new location, or alternatively call 132 701 to speak to an operator.

arrangementElements.title: Road and lane closures

arrangementElements.html:
Reduced speed limits will be in effect on the following roads to ensure the safety of cyclists sharing roads with motorists:

ST IVES TO TERREY HILLS

FROM 6AM TO 11.30AM

  • 60km/h speed limit reduction on Mona Vale Road between Wildflower Gardens at St Ives to Myoora Road at Terrey Hills (eastbound)

FROM 6AM TO 2PM

  • 60km/h speed limit reduction on Mona Vale Road from McCarrs Creek Road at Terrey Hills to opposite the Wildflower Garden at St Ives (westbound)

KU-RING-GAI CHASE - FROM 6AM TO 1PM

  • 60km/h speed limit reduction on West Head Road between Liberator General San Martin Drive and West Head Picnic Area (both directions)

MT COLAH - FROM 6AM TO 10AM

  • 60km/h speed limit reduction on Bobbin Head Road between Park Gates and Kalkari Visitor Centre (eastbound)

MT COLAH - FROM 5:30AM TO 9:30AM

  • 40km/h speed limit reduction on Old Pacific Highway between Pacific Highway and Belmont Parade (both directions)

TERREY HILLS - FROM 6AM TO 11.30AM 

  • 40km/h speed limit reduction on McCarr's Creek Road between West Head Road and Liberator General San Martin Drive (westbound)

properties.publicTransport: Major trackwork is taking place on City Circle, metro and T1 North Shore Line. Race day tickets include travel on public transport. Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
Autumn Carnival: Family Stakes Day at Rosehill Gardens Racecourse. First race at 12.30pm, last race from 5:55pm.

Traffic may be heavier than usual on James Ruse Dr and Hassall St as attendees arrive and leave the racecourse.

Motorists should allow extra travel time and be aware of increased pedestrian activity on local roads in the area.

Special event clearway parking restrictions will be in effect at the following locations:

  • From 6am to 6pm: Grand Avenue from 100m Prior to Gate 1 Australian Turf Club Rosehill Gardens to opposite Grand Avenue North (southern side).

Clearways are strict no-parking zones for all vehicle types and apply even to local residents and TfNSW Mobility Scheme Permit holders. Check signs carefully as vehicles left in clearways will be towed and a fee applies. If your vehicle is towed, you can search your vehicle registration at the Find My Car website to find its new location, or alternatively call 132 701 to speak to an operator.

properties.publicTransport: Additional route 199 buses will also run towards Manly and Mona Vale after the game to help you get home. Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
NRL: Sea Eagles v Roosters at 4 Pines Park in Brookvale. Prelim matches from 5.30pm. NRL kick-off at 8pm and full time around 9.50pm.

Traffic will be heavier than usual as fans travel to and from the match. Allow extra travel time on roads approaching the stadium, including Pittwater Rd, Old Pittwater Rd, Warringah Rd and Beacon Hill Rd.

Motorists should plan to arrive early as parking will be in high demand, and also be aware of increased pedestrian activity on local roads.

NOTE: There is no parking on the southern side of Federal Pde between Pine Ave and Alfred Rd permitted from 8pm the night before the match until the crowd has safely cleared the stadium after full time on game day.

properties.publicTransport: Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
NBL Championship Series Game 3: Sydney Kings v Adelaide 36ers at Qudos Bank Arena on Sunday 29 March from 2.15pm. Game 5 will be played if required.

  • Traffic delays are expected on roads approaching Olympic Park as basketball fans make their way to and from the match. 
  • Motorists should carpool, plan to arrive early and allow extra travel time on roads including the M4 Mwy, Homebush Bay Dr, Concord Rd, Lane Cove Rd, Parramatta Rd, Centenary Dr and Silverwater Rd.
  • If you need to drive, pre-book parking online in advance to secure a space. Follow the directions of staff and have your parking ticket ready to show at the car park entry. Note that P6 car park will be closed due to Easter Show infrastructure.
  • Use Silverwater Rd, Holker St and Hill Rd to access precinct car parks and avoid heavy traffic expected on Homebush Bay Drive and Underwood Rd. 

Sydney Olympic Park Authority will close local roads in the precinct to ensure pedestrian safety during the event and to accommodate additional transport services. For road closure details, view the interactive map.

roads.mainStreet: M4 Motorway, Homebush Bay Drive, Parramatta Road

properties.publicTransport: Match tickets include travel on transport. Buses will divert while roads are closed after the game. Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
NRL: Eels v Dragons at CommBank Stadium. NSW Cup game at 1.35pm, NRL kick off at 4.05pm, full time at 5.50pm

With thousands expected, traffic will be heavy on roads approaching Parramatta and nearby car parks are expected to be in high demand.

Allow extra travel time on roads including Parramatta Road, Church Street, Victoria Road, James Ruse Drive and Pennant Hills Road. 

Motorists should also exercise caution as pedestrian activity will be high on roads between Parramatta Interchange and the stadium. 

Drivers are reminded that light rail services are running through Parramatta CBD. Trams can’t stop quickly or easily – so exercise extra caution when driving in the area. 

Road closures 

Roads around the stadium will close from 5.50pm to 6.20pm approx. to allow crowds to safely leave the venue. Roads will re-open once crowds have cleared the area. 

  • O’Connell Street from Grose Street to George Street 
  • Ross Street from O’Connell Street to Trott Street 
  • Victoria Road from O’Connell Street to Villiers Street 

While closures are in place motorists are encouraged to avoid driving through Parramatta CBD and detour around the closures via Grose Street, Brickfield Street, Victoria Road and Wilde Avenue in both directions.

properties.publicTransport: Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
Super Netball Double Header at Qudos Bank Arena. Game 1 - Giants v Melbourne Vixens: 3pm. Game 2 - NSW Swifts v Melbourne Mavericks: 5pm, finish at 6.30pm

Traffic delays are expected as netball fans make their way to Olympic Park.

Motorists should carpool and allow extra travel time on roads including the M4 Mwy, Homebush Bay Dr, Concord Rd, Lane Cove Rd, Parramatta Rd, Centenary Dr and Silverwater Rd.

If you have to drive, pre-book parking online in advance to secure a space, available until 11:59pm the night before the event unless sold out prior. If you have pre-booked your parking, follow the directions of staff and have your parking ticket ready to show at the car park entry.

Use Silverwater Rd, Holker St and Hill Rd to access precinct car parks and avoid heavy traffic expected on Homebush Bay Drive and Underwood Rd. 

Sydney Olympic Park Authority may close local roads in the precinct to ensure pedestrian safety during the event and to accommodate additional transport services. For road closure details, view the interactive map.

roads.mainStreet: M4 Motorway, Homebush Bay Drive, Parramatta Road

properties.publicTransport: Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
A-League Men: Macarthur FC v Newcastle Jets on Thursday 2 April. Kick-off at 7.35pm, full time around 9.30pm.

Traffic may be heavier than usual as fans travel to the match in the already busy evening peak. Allow extra travel time on roads including Pembroke Rd, Rose Payten Dr and Leumeah Rd.

Motorists should also be aware of increased pedestrian activity in the area.

roads.mainStreet: Pembroke Road and Rose Payten Drive

properties.publicTransport: Match tickets include travel on public transport. Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
NRL: Rabbitohs v Bulldogs at Accor Stadium. NSW Cup from 1.45pm then NRL kick-off at 4.05pm. Full time around 5.50pm.

  • Olympic Park will be extremely busy on Good Friday with more than 100,000 people expected at the Easter Show in addition to up to 30,000 footy fans.
  • Footy fans are urged to plan their trip and consider the public transport options to avoid the heavy traffic and demand for parking.  
  • If you must drive, it is essential that you pre-book your parking online to secure a space – available until 11:59pm the night before the event unless sold out prior. Please note P5 & P6 parking will not be available due to Easter Show infrastructure.
  • Motorists should carpool and allow plenty of extra travel time on roads including the M4 Mwy, Homebush Bay Dr, Concord Rd, Lane Cove Rd, Parramatta Rd, Centenary Dr and Silverwater Rd.
  • If you have pre-booked your parking, follow the directions of staff and have your parking ticket ready to show at the car park entry.
  • If you haven't pre-booked parking, do not drive to Olympic Park. You will need to catch public transport as there will be no parking on-site. You can also consider parking at a nearby commuter car park and continuing your trip by train or bus to Olympic Park, at no extra cost. 

Road closures and changes to parklands will be in place within the Sydney Olympic Park Precinct before, during and after the Easter Show for event set up and pack down. 

Check out the Sydney Olympic Park Authority interactive map for more details. For detailed closure and access changes in the precinct see the Olympic Park Community page.

arrangementElements.html:
Road closures 

Road closures and changes to parklands will be in place within the Sydney Olympic Park Precinct before, during and after the Easter Show for event set up and pack down. 

Check out the Sydney Olympic Park Authority interactive map for more details. For detailed closure and access changes in the precinct see the Olympic Park Community page. 

Monday 9 March at 1.30am to Tuesday 21 April at 1.30am:   

  • Olympic Boulevard 100m north of Dawn Fraser Avenue adjacent to Cathy Freeman Park amenities, to 200m south of Edwin Flack Avenue outside Qudos Bank Arena.  

Thursday 19 March at 1.30am to Tuesday 21 April at 1.30am:   

  • Australia Avenue from Murray Rose Avenue to Kevin Coombs Avenue. 
  • Showground Road from Murray Rose Avenue to Grand Parade. 
  • Grand Parade from Olympic Boulevard to Australia Avenue.  

Thursday 2 April at 1.30am to Tuesday 14 April at 4am:  

  • Kevin Coombs Avenue from Australia Avenue to Pondage Link. 
  • Marjorie Jackson Parkway from Wentworth Common Carpark to Australia Avenue. 
  • No right turn available onto Holker Busway from Marjorie Jackson Parkway. 
  • No left turn available from Holker Busway onto Marjorie Jackson Parkway. 
  • Olympic Boulevard from Kevin Coombs Avenue to Sarah Durack Avenue. 
  • Showground Road from Murray Rose Avenue to Dawn Fraser Avenue. 
  • Murray Rose Avenue from Showground Road to Gate 15 and Park Street. 
  • Dawn Fraser Avenue from Edwin Flack Avenue to Showground Road (accessible drop-off/pick-up and Show drop-off/pick-up – Edwin Flack Avenue to Fig Grove). 
  • Dawn Fraser Avenue from Showground Road to Park Street. 
  • Dawn Fraser Avenue from Park Street to Australia Avenue (eastbound only). 
  • Park Street from Dawn Fraser Avenue to Herb Elliott Avenue (northbound only). 
  • Murray Rose Avenue from Park Street to the west side of the entrance or carpark P6D. 
  • Australia Avenue from Dawn Fraser Avenue to Murray Rose Avenue. 
  • Shirley Strickland Avenue from Rod Laver Drive to Olympic Boulevard. 
  • Shirley Strickland Avenue from Olympic Boulevard to the M4 Exit (P4 car park). 
  • Rod Laver Drive from Shirley Strickland Avenue to the Tennis Centre. 
  • Shane Gould Avenue at P2A Car Park. (eastern direction).

roads.mainStreet: M4 Motorway, Homebush Bay Drive, Parramatta Road, Centenary Drive

properties.otherAdvice:
Increased motor and pedestrian traffic due to the Kurri Kurri Nostalgia Festival from Friday 27 to Sunday 29 March.


Local Road impacts as follows:

Lang Street closed -Hampden Street to Kurri Community Centre

• Barton Street closed from Saturday 28 March 21:00 to Sunday 29 March 17:00

• Lang Street closed - from the Mitchell Avenue roundabout to approx. 30 meters west of Allworth Street.

• Allworth Street closed - from the laneway at rear of Barton Street businesses (2O meters south of Lang St) to the laneway at rear of Coles (20 meters north of Lang Street).

• Hampden Street closed - from the laneway 2O meters south of Barton Street to Brunker Street.

• Barton St closed - from public carpark to Allworth Street

properties.publicTransport: Match tickets include travel on transport for both games. Buses will divert while roads are closed after the games. Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
NRL matches at Commbank Stadium in Parramatta over the Easter long weekend:

  • Friday 3 April - Panthers v Storm. NSW Cup from 5.25pm. NRL kick-off at 8pm, full time at 9.45pm. Road closures from 10pm to 10.30pm.
  • Monday 6 April - Eels v West Tigers. NSW Cup from 1.40pm. NRL kick-off at 4.05pm and full time around 5.50pm. Road closures from 6pm to 6.30pm.

With thousands of fans expected at each match, traffic will be heavy on roads approaching Parramatta and car parks in the area will be in high demand.

Motorists should carpool with their ground, plan to arrive early and allow extra travel time on roads including Parramatta Road, Church Street, Victoria Road, James Ruse Drive and Pennant Hills Road. 

Motorists should also exercise caution as pedestrian activity will be high on roads between Parramatta Interchange and the stadium.

Drivers are reminded that light rail services are running through Parramatta CBD. Trams can’t stop quickly or easily – so exercise extra caution when driving in the area. 


Road closures 

Roads around the stadium will close from 10-10.30pm and from 6-6.30pm on Friday and Monday respectively to allow crowds to safely leave the venue after each match. Roads will re-open once crowds have cleared the area. 

  • O’Connell Street from Grose Street to George Street 
  • Ross Street from O’Connell Street to Trott Street 
  • Victoria Road from O’Connell Street to Villiers Street 

While closures are in place, motorists are encouraged to avoid driving through Parramatta CBD and detour around the closures via Grose Street, Brickfield Street, Victoria Road and Wilde Avenue in both directions.

properties.publicTransport: Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
NRL: Dragons v Cowboys at Jubilee Stadium. Kick-off at 5.30pm, full time at 7.30pm

Traffic will be heavier than usual in both directions on the Princes Hwy approaching Kogarah as fans travel to the stadium.

Local roads around the stadium may close ahead of match start including Jubilee Ave, Park St and English St. Roads will re-open once crowds have cleared the area.

Fans driving to the game should allow extra time for parking and be aware of increased pedestrian activity on local roads.

roads.mainStreet: Jubilee Avenue, English Street, Park Street

properties.publicTransport: Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
A-League Central Coast Mariners double header at polytec Stadium in Gosford. Women v Sydney FC from 2.15pm followed by the Men v Perth glory at 5pm. Finish approx. 7pm.

Traffic will be heavier than usual on roads approaching the stadium as fans travel to and from the match. Allow extra travel time and use local parking. 

Dane Drive will be closed from 1.30pm between the Central Coast Highway and Donnison Street and will reopen once crowds have cleared the area.

A parade will be held requiring a full closure of Kendall St (Mid Western Hwy). Motorists should follow detour signs.

The following local road closures will also be in place:

Bartlett Avenue – between Darling & Brisbane Sts

Brisbane Street – between Bartlett Ave & Railway Ln

Macquarie Street - between Rowston Ln and Brougham St

Vaux Street – between Doust Ln & Lachlan Sts

Lachlan Street – closed at Vaux St
properties.diversions: Detour (all vehicles both directions) College Dr, Liverpool St and Lachlan St.

properties.publicTransport: Race day tickets include travel on all public transport modes. Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
Autumn Carnival: Championship Day 1 at Royal Randwick Racecourse. First race at 12.25pm, last race from 5.50pm.

Traffic may be heavier than usual on Anzac Pde, Doncaster Ave, High St, Alison Rd and Darley Rd as crowds travel to and from the event.

Motorists should allow extra travel time and be aware of increased pedestrian activity on local roads.

Special event clearways will be in place at the following times and locations: 

  • 4am – 7pm: Darley Road between Alison Road and the Grand Drive roundabout (western side) 
  • 8am – 7pm: Ascot Street between Doncaster Avenue and the roundabout at the entry to Randwick Racecourse (both sides) 
  • 3pm – 7pm: High Street between Gate Two Avenue and Anzac Parade (southern side) 

Clearways are strict no-parking zones for all vehicle types and apply even to local residents and TfNSW Mobility Scheme Permit holders. Check signs carefully as vehicles left in clearways will be towed and a fee applies. If your vehicle is towed, you can search your vehicle registration at the Find My Car website to find its new location, or alternatively call 132 701 to speak to an operator.

properties.publicTransport: Match tickets include travel on trains. Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
NRL: Sharks v Warriors at Ocean Protect Stadium in Woolooware. Kick-off at 2pm, full time around 3.50pm.

Road closures will be in place on Captain Cook Dr from approximately 10 minutes before kick-off and remain in place until crowds have cleared around 30 minutes after full-time.

Traffic on roads approaching the stadium will be heavier than usual as fans travel to and from the match. Allow extra travel time on Captain Cook Dr, Woolooware Rd and Gannons Rd. 

Motorists should also be aware of increased pedestrian activity in the area.

properties.publicTransport: Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
NRL: Knights v Raiders at McDonald Jones Stadium. NSW Cup kick-off at 1.35pm followed by NRL kick-off at 4.05pm. Full time at approx 5.40pm.

Traffic may be heavier than usual as fans travel to and from the match. Allow extra travel time on Turton Rd, Griffiths Rd, Newcastle Rd, Lambton Rd and Bridges Rd through Broadmeadow while fans are making their way to and from the stadium.

For passenger drop-off, use the allocated 10-minute loading zone in the McDonald Jones Stadium car park. Entry is via the driveway off Turton Road.

Motorists should allow extra travel time and be aware of increased pedestrian activity on local roads.﻿

properties.publicTransport: Light rail and buses run regularly to Moore Park. Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
The Sydney Family Show will be held at The Entertainment Quarter from Thursday 2 to Sunday 19 April. Show starts at 10am daily and will finish between 3pm and 5pm depending on the day.

  • Traffic is expected to be heavier than usual on Anzac Pde, Eastern Distributor, Moore Park Rd, Cleveland St and Lang Rd approaching Moore Park as families travel to and from the Show each day. Motorists should allow extra travel time in the area and be aware of increased pedestrian activity on local roads.
  • The precinct will be particularly busy while events are held at neighbouring Allianz Stadium, Sydney Cricket Ground and Hordern Pavilion so allow plenty of extra travel time as parking will be in demand on these days.
  • Driver Ave will be partially closed outside the stadiums at various times on game days on 17, 18 and 19 April.

Special event clearways will be in effect on roads in and around Moore Park on 17, 18 and 19 April (game days) while the Show takes place so check signs carefully before parking as vehicles left in clearways will be towed and a fee applies.

Clearways are strict no-parking zones for all vehicle types, and apply even to local residents and TfNSW Mobility Scheme Permit holders. Check signs carefully as vehicles left in clearways will be towed and a fee applies. If your vehicle is towed, you can search your vehicle registration at the Find My Car website to find its new location, or alternatively call 132 701 to speak to an operator.

arrangementElements.html:
ROAD CLOSURES

Driver Ave at Moore Park will be partially closed during events at nearby SCG and Allianz Stadium on the following dates: 

  • Friday 17 April from 3.30pm 
  • Saturday 18 April from 4.30pm 
  • Sunday 19 April from 11am

SPECIAL EVENT CLEARWAYS

Clearways are strict no-parking zones for all vehicle types, and apply even to local residents and TfNSW Mobility Scheme Permit holders. Check signs carefully as vehicles left in clearways will be towed and a fee applies. If your vehicle is towed, you can search your vehicle registration at the Find My Car website to find its new location, or alternatively call 132 701 to speak to an operator.


Special event clearways will be in place on roads in Moore Park for events at neighbouring SCG and Allianz Stadium on sections of Albion Street, Lang Road and Moore Park Road, Cleveland Street and Fitzroy Street.  

Clearways will be in effect on the following dates and times, check signs carefully to avoid being towed: 

  • Friday 17 April from 6am to midnight for the Super Rugby and AFL Derby
  • Saturday 18 April from 6am to 11pm for the A-League Sydney FC v Perth Glory
  • Sunday 19 April from 6am to 7pm for NRL Roosters v Knights
arrangementElements.title: Road Closures and Special Event Clearways

roads.mainStreet: Moore Park Road, Anzac Parade, Lang Road, Eastern Distributor

webLinks.linkText: Sydney Olympic Park Authority - Community Impacts Easter Show 2025

properties.publicTransport: Travel on public transport is included in tickets to the Show. Express trains and Major Event Buses will run daily. Regular route buses 525, 526, 533 and N81 in Olympic Park will use different stops while roads are closed. Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
Hundreds of thousands of people are expected to visit the annual Sydney Royal Easter Show over 12 days at Sydney Olympic Park from Thursday 2 to Monday 13 April. 

  • Motorists should allow extra travel time on roads approaching the precinct including the M4 Mwy, Homebush Bay Dr, Concord Rd, Lane Cove Rd, Parramatta Rd, Centenary Dr and Silverwater Rd throughout the show. 
  • If you have pre-booked parking, follow directions of staff and have your parking ticket ready to show at the car park entry.
  • If you haven't pre-booked parking do not drive to Olympic Park. You will need to catch public transport as there will be no parking on-site. You can also consider parking at a nearby commuter car park and continuing your trip by train or bus to Olympic Park, at no extra cost.  

Road closures and changes to parklands are in place within the Sydney Olympic Park Precinct from Monday 9 March until Tuesday 21 April before, during and after the Easter Show including sections of Dawn Fraser Ave, Olympic Blvd, Showground Rd, Murray Rose Ave, Kevin Coombs Ave, Marjorie Jackson Dwy, Shirley Strickland Ave, Rod Laver Dr. Check out the Sydney Olympic Park Authority interactive map for more details

arrangementElements.html:
Road closures

Road closures and changes to parklands will be in place within the Sydney Olympic Park Precinct before, during and after the Show for event set up and pack down. Check out the Sydney Olympic Park Authority interactive map for more details. 

Monday 9 March at 1.30am to Tuesday 21 April at 1.30am:  

  • Olympic Boulevard 100m north of Dawn Fraser Avenue adjacent to Cathy Freeman Park amenities, to 200m south of Edwin Flack Avenue outside Qudos Bank Arena. 

Thursday 19 March at 1.30am to Tuesday 21 April at 1.30am:  

  • Australia Avenue from Murray Rose Avenue to Kevin Coombs Avenue.
  • Showground Road from Murray Rose Avenue to Grand Parade.
  • Grand Parade from Olympic Boulevard to Australia Avenue. 

Thursday 2 April at 1.30am to Tuesday 14 April at 4am: 

  • Kevin Coombs Avenue from Australia Avenue to Pondage Link.
  • Marjorie Jackson Parkway from Wentworth Common Carpark to Australia Avenue.
  • No right turn available onto Holker Busway from Marjorie Jackson Parkway.
  • No left turn available from Holker Busway onto Marjorie Jackson Parkway.
  • Olympic Boulevard from Kevin Coombs Avenue to Sarah Durack Avenue.
  • Showground Road from Murray Rose Avenue to Dawn Fraser Avenue.
  • Murray Rose Avenue from Showground Road to Gate 15 and Park Street.
  • Dawn Fraser Avenue from Edwin Flack Avenue to Showground Road (accessible drop-off/pick-up and Show drop-off/pick-up – Edwin Flack Avenue to Fig Grove).
  • Dawn Fraser Avenue from Showground Road to Park Street.
  • Dawn Fraser Avenue from Park Street to Australia Avenue (eastbound only).
  • Park Street from Dawn Fraser Avenue to Herb Elliott Avenue (northbound only).
  • Murray Rose Avenue from Park Street to the west side of the entrance or carpark P6D.
  • Australia Avenue from Dawn Fraser Avenue to Murray Rose Avenue.
  • Shirley Strickland Avenue from Rod Laver Drive to Olympic Boulevard.
  • Shirley Strickland Avenue from Olympic Boulevard to the M4 Exit (P4 car park).
  • Rod Laver Drive from Shirley Strickland Avenue to the Tennis Centre.
  • Shane Gould Avenue at P2A Car Park. (eastern direction). 

Saturday 4 April at 1pm to Saturday 4 April at 9pm: 

  • Shirley Strickland Avenue from Olympic Boulevard to Bannon Bridge 

Sunday 5 April at 10am to Sunday 5 April at 6pm: 

  • Shirley Strickland Avenue from Olympic Boulevard to Bannon Bridge 

Saturday 11 April at 1pm to Saturday 11 April at 9pm: 

  • Shirley Strickland Avenue from Olympic Boulevard to Bannon Bridge

properties.otherAdvice:
Easter Monday Race Day at Rosehill Gardens Racecourse. First race at 12.50pm, last race from 4.55pm.

Traffic may be heavier than usual on James Ruse Dr and Hassall St as attendees arrive and leave the racecourse.

Motorists should allow extra travel time and be aware of increased pedestrian activity on local roads in the area.

properties.publicTransport: Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
April shows at the TikTok Entertainment Centre:

  • Thursday 16 April: In Conversation with Pamela Anderson from 7pm to 9pm
  • Friday 17 April: Guo Degang & Yuqian Cross Talk Show from 7.30pm to 10.30pm
  • Sunday 19 April: ITZY from 7pm to 10pm
  • Thursday 30 April to Saturday 2 May: Guy Sebastian. Shows start at 7pm nightly.

Traffic may be heavier than usual as attendees travel to and from the event.

Motorists should allow extra travel time and be aware of increased pedestrian activity on local roads.
